name: Roman Empire single-intent (the 'and' trap)
description: Long single-intent question that contains the word 'and'. The pre-Phase-4 regex override saw 'and' and allowed multi-winner; the new bid-overlap penalty looks at reasoning text, finds heavy overlap between search-agent and history-agent, and shrinks history-agent's confidence so the dominance gap opens. This is the demo case where the old system was actively wrong.

task.content: tell me about the Roman Empire and how it fell

bids.reasoning: I will search the web and find authoritative information about the Roman Empire including its founding rise and eventual fall
I will explain the Roman Empire including its rise growth and the various factors that led to its eventual fall

expected.rationale: Heavy reasoning overlap between search and history; history-agent shrinks via overlap penalty; dominance gap opens; fast path picks search.
expected.postOverlapBehavior: Fast path fires; LLM not consulted; single winner.
